input.user_input: My client is planning to acquire a competitor. How do I assess whether the deal will trigger HSR antitrust filing thresholds?

actual:
To assess whether your client’s planned acquisition will trigger a filing requirement under the Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR) Antitrust Improvements Act, you must evaluate the transaction against three primary tests: the Commerce Test, the Size of Transaction Test, and the Size of Person Test. You must also consider whether any exemptions apply. 

Because the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) adjusts HSR thresholds annually based on changes in the gross national product (usually effective in late February or early March), you must use the thresholds in effect at the time the transaction closes. The figures below reflect the 2024 thresholds.

Here is the step-by-step framework for your assessment:

### 1. Identify the Ultimate Parent Entities (UPEs)
Before applying the financial tests, you must identify the "Acquiring Person" and the "Acquired Person." Under HSR rules, a "Person" is the Ultimate Parent Entity (UPE) and all entities it controls.
